title: engine doctor QA + Proposed-story inbox lines disappear when no sprint is active
summary: engine/tools/doctor/project-doctor.ts:1943-1948 — `formatBacklog` early-returns from the no-active-sprint branch (`if (report.active === undefined)`). The Recent commits, Follow-ups, QA inbox, and Proposed-story inbox sections (all at the bottom of the function, lines 1973-2026) never get to render in that branch. Surfaced post-S099 merge: S099 is archived, no sprint is active locally, and even though 8 GDP-* files sit in backlog/proposed-stories/, the doctor output skips straight from `Backlog: 95 sprint file(s), 95 archived. No sprint is currently active.` to the Recommendations section. The same affects S093 QA-DOCTOR-INBOX: with no active sprint, QA tickets in the inbox are invisible from the doctor. This is exactly the WORST time for those signals to disappear — between sprints is when the dev terminal should be planning the next one, and the doctor is the canonical 'what needs my attention' view. The S099 AGF-DOCTOR-PROPOSED-STORIES unit test (tests/unit/doctor-proposed-inbox.test.ts:23) only covers the active-sprint path (`status: 'active'`), which is why this slipped through.
repro: From a fresh `origin/main` checkout (HEAD 6d4dc1f, post-S099 merge), confirm no sprint JSON has `"status": "active"`: `grep -l '"status": "active"' backlog/sprints/*.sprint.json` returns nothing.
Confirm there's content in the proposed-story inbox: `ls backlog/proposed-stories/*.story-proposal.json | wc -l` — currently 8 files (GDP-2026-05-20-003 through -010).

Scroll to the `Backlog:` section. Observe the entire output of that section is just one line: `Backlog: 95 sprint file(s), 95 archived. No sprint is currently active.` Then the doctor jumps straight to the Recommendations block.

Notice the missing lines that the S099 AGF-DOCTOR-PROPOSED-STORIES acceptance promises: `Proposed-story inbox: 8 pending (...)` and the per-proposal listing. Also missing: the S093 QA-DOCTOR-INBOX line, plus Recent commits and Follow-ups (S087 + S086 stories).
expected: When the doctor runs against a project with no active sprint, the Backlog section still surfaces QA inbox + Proposed-story inbox + Recent commits + Follow-ups beneath the `No sprint is currently active.` summary. Concretely: with 8 GDP-* files in `backlog/proposed-stories/`, the line `  Proposed-story inbox: 8 pending (...)` should appear regardless of whether a sprint is active. Same for QA inbox per S093.
actual: `formatBacklog` returns early at line 1948 in the no-active-sprint branch, so every line below — including the QA inbox (S093), Proposed-story inbox (S099), Recent commits (S087), and Follow-ups (S086) sections — is skipped. The dev terminal sees a one-line `Backlog:` summary at the exact moment those signals matter most (between-sprint planning).
